description: Ambleside Online Year 2 — Charlotte Mason living books curriculum for grades 1-2. Longer books, richer narrations, and introduction to biography and natural history.

books.title: The Wind in the Willows
books.author: Kenneth Grahame
books.subject: living_books
books.notes: Rich in description of nature and friendship. Excellent for narration of character motivation — 'Why do you think Mole left his home that spring morning?'

books.title: Robinson Crusoe (abridged)
books.author: Daniel Defoe
books.subject: living_books
books.notes: Use a good children's abridgment. Natural narration prompt: 'How did Crusoe solve the problem of food?' Themes of Providence and trust in God run throughout.

books.title: Pilgrim's Progress (children's adaptation)
books.author: John Bunyan
books.subject: saints
books.notes: The greatest allegory in the English language. Ask: 'What do you think this place represents in our own lives?' Connects beautifully to catechism and virtue formation.

books.title: The Bird Book (Birds Every Child Should Know)
books.author: Neltje Blanchan
books.subject: nature_study
books.notes: Pair with actual bird watching. Before reading about a bird, ask the child to describe any birds they have seen. After reading, go look for the bird together.

books.title: Wild Animals I Have Known
books.author: Ernest Thompson Seton
books.subject: nature_study
books.notes: Seton pioneered nature writing from the animal's perspective. Builds deep sympathy for creation. Ask: 'What did you notice about how Lobo led his pack?'

books.title: This Country of Ours
books.author: Henrietta Elizabeth Marshall
books.subject: history
books.notes: Narrative American history from discovery through the Civil War. One chapter per sitting. Excellent narration material — each chapter has a clear protagonist and conflict.

books.title: Fifty Famous Stories Retold
books.author: James Baldwin
books.subject: history
books.notes: Continue from Year 1 if not completed, or revisit favorites. Excellent for copywork selection — Baldwin's prose is clear and memorable.

books.title: Understood Betsy
books.author: Dorothy Canfield Fisher
books.subject: living_books
books.notes: If not read in Year 1, excellent here. Elizabeth Ann's transformation mirrors the Charlotte Mason philosophy of education — discovery over instruction.

books.title: Trial and Triumph: Stories from Church History
books.author: Richard Hannula
books.subject: saints
books.notes: Continue working through this rich resource. Pair stories with the liturgical calendar when possible — read about a saint near their feast day.

books.title: The Woodpeckers
books.author: Fannie Hardy Eckstorm
books.subject: nature_study
books.notes: A detailed, loving study of woodpeckers by a careful naturalist. Use alongside actual observation. Ask: 'What do woodpeckers eat and how do they find it?'

books.title: A Child's History of the World
books.author: V. M. Hillyer
books.subject: history
books.notes: Continue from Year 1 or begin if not started. Narrative history that treats children as capable of engaging with real events and real people.
